rulings.Case Title: VIMLABEN PATEL VS. NARENDRA SHARMA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT NARENDRA SHARMA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication. Defendant Sharma's motion for summary judgment or adjudication is denied. Defendant fails to maintain his summary judgment burden per Code of Civil Procedure, § 437c(p)(2). The gist of the complaint is that plaintiff agreed to lend $15,000 to defendant pursuant to a promissory note and plaintiff ultimately lent $16,000 to defendant per the same terms. (Complaint, 7-8.) Defendant's paltry separate statement of undisputed material facts fails to show that plaintiff's claim "cannot be established" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, § 437c(p)(2). Defendant "does not deny receiving two separate loans from plaintiff, for $13,000 and $3,000" (Reply at 1), and the signature on the promissory note appears identical to the signature on his declaration. There exist issues of fact regarding whether and when Defendant executed the promissory note, the terms and conditions of the parties' agreement, and whether the promissory note was supposed to apply to the $16,000 loan. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: HORNBLOWER YACHTS, LLC. ET AL VS. SITZMANN MORRIS & LAVIS ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2019, Line 3, Plaintiff'S Motion To Strike Defendants' Supplemental Expert Disclosures Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to klgallo@disoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLIOT TAYLOR ET AL VS. MICHAEL CALLAHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Seal Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2019, Line 5, CROSS COMPLAINANTs COLTON CALLAHAN, MICHAEL CALLAHAN's Motion To Seal Second Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainants' motion to seal portions of the Second Amended Cross-Complaint is granted. Contrary to Plaintiffs' knee-jerk opposition, the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551, and is supported by a sufficient evidentiary showing. (See Callahan Decl. 2-4; Supp. Callahan Decl., Ex. A.) The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the movants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LORAN SIMON VS. UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order To Arbitrate And Stay Action By Defendants Community Initiatives And Peter Williamson
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, November 1, 2019, Line 10, # 2 - DEFENDANTS COMMUNITY INITIATIVES, PETER WILLIAMSON's Hearing On Petition For Order To Arbitrate And Stay Action By Defendants Community Initiatives And Peter Williamson. Defendants Community Initiatives and Peter Williamson's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. The broad arbitration agreement with USF applies to plaintiff's claims. USF is clearly entitled to compel arbitration. The non-signatories can also compel arbitration based upon estoppel and agency. "[A] nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claim when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are intimately founded in and intertwined with the underlying contract obligations. The doctrine applies where the claims are based on the same facts and are inherently inseparable from the arbitrable claims against signatory defendants." (Garcia v. Pexco, LLC (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 782, 786 (internal quotations and citations omitted); see also Vasquez v. San Miguel Produce, Inc. (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 810, 816 [a "co-employer relationship and identity of interest with regard to their mutual employees allows [non-signatory defendants] to compel arbitration of an employment dispute"]; Martinez v. Scott Specialty Gases, Inc. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1236, 1245 [agent can compel arbitration].) Plaintiff's claims regarding his employment after 10/6/16 are inextricably intertwined with his pre-10/6/16 claims and plaintiff must arbitrate the entire action. Plaintiff admits that he resumed his prior position and plaintiff's alleged "new employers" after 10/6/16 (e.g. SFTR and SFUSD) were plaintiff's joint employer prior to that time. (Compl. 58; see also, e.g., id. 59-61 [alleging that between October and November 2016, "Plaintiff continued experiencing, and reporting issues of," labor code violations and denial of promised benefits, and that between December 2016 to May 2017, he "exercised a series of protected activities," including participating "as a witness in the investigations that followed these activities, including issues from the June 2014 through September 30, 2016 employment."].) The FAA requires arbitration of the Bane Act claim. (Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 308, 323 ["[S]ection 2 of the FAA "preempts any state rule discriminating on its face against arbitration-for example, a 'law prohibit[ing] outright the arbitration of a particular type of claim.'"] [holding that trial court erred in denying petition to compel arbitration of Bane Act claims].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
